pages.full_text:
Simon + Eve walked into Lahinch. We visited the woollen shop. Eve bought an orange + blue silk/lambswool cardigan. [?Thibrdgum] [?bought sandwieder] We went back to the van, sorted the washing + set off for Moher the __Cliffs of Moher__. Lovely countryside. Thatched [?eat] cottages. B+Bs. Sea-view/ocean view/island view/Atlantic view.
      Boys wanted to walk on dangerous paths. Woman harpist singing about 5 sons. Man harpist. Man piper. Sheer cliffs to the sea + tower
      We went to the music shop at Dolin where John bought a pipe.
      At __Aillwee__ Mountain we went into the Cave. Remains of 2 bear pits – European brown bear extinct in 800s. Straw stalactites (1 inch = 80 years) one stalagmite 8,000 years old. Discovered 1940, opened as a private family in 1976, 5 years ago they blasted a second passageway out. Simon bought an Australian hat.
      Tea on the __Burren__ – amazing limestone plateaus with clefts [?grikes] – 1 m. deep + v. straight.
pages.notes: Top of page is a continuation from previous page. 'Dolin' likely 'Doolin' — diary spells it 'Dolin'. Some interlinear annotations above 'blue silk/lambswool cardigan' line are partially illegible; best reading given. '[?grikes]' — geological term for cleft in limestone pavement, likely correct reading. Year 1996 confirmed by right-hand page heading 'Ireland August 1996'.

pages.full_text:
__Ireland August 1996__

1. Dun Laoghaire
2. Glendalough
3. Kildare
4. Kilkenny
5. Cashel
6. Dingle
7. Dunquin / Dún Chaoin
8. Great Blasket
9. Tralee
10. Shannon Ferry
11. Lahinch
12. Ennistimon
13. Rocks of Moher
14. The Burren / Aillwee Mtn.
15. Clonmacnoise
16. Lough Ree
17. Killyleagh
18. Knowth
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn outline map of Ireland with numbered locations (1–18) plotted and a red route line tracing the journey. Route appears to start in the east (around Dublin/Dun Laoghaire area), travel south through Kilkenny/Cashel, west to Dingle peninsula (nos. 6–8), north along west coast through Shannon/Lahinch/Burren, then northeast to Clonmacnoise/Lough Ree, and back east toward Killyleagh/Knowth. Small pictograms (castles, boats, etc.) mark some sites.
pages.notes: This page functions as a trip index/route summary page rather than a prose narrative. Heading 'Ireland August 1996' is underlined. Route line on map drawn in red. No. 13 reads 'Rocks of Moher' (cf. left page 'Cliffs of Moher' — diary uses both names). No. 7 gives both English and Irish spellings: 'Dunquin / Dún Chaoin'. No. 17 reads 'Killyleagh' — could also be 'Killyleagh' (Co. Down, Northern Ireland).
